title: how do i put multiple commands in entrypoint?
body:
i am looking for something like this: 
podman run --entrypoint "/sbin/init && /path/to/my/script.sh"

comments.body:
Hi @imperialguy , Thanks for opening the issue.

I think you can only choose one executable but you could provide other commands as arguments.

For example my image contains `sh` and `bash`.

So i do it like
```console
podman run -it --rm --entrypoint "/bin/sh" test -c 'echo hello && echo world'
```

similarly if your init accepts scripts as argument you could provide it as arguments.

> 

I don't think `podman run` has any option called `-c`, Is `test` the name of the script? Did you mean this? 

```
podman run -it --rm --entrypoint "/bin/sh test -c 'echo hello && echo world'"
```
I don't think the above works either. Also, `/sbin/init` basically is a shortcut to call `systemd`. What I want is the ability to run commands right after `systemd` initializes. Any ideas?

@imperialguy `-c` is not a podman option but argument to my entrypoint binary which is `/bin/sh`. and `test` is my image name.

I'll use `alpine` so its not confusing, try below command as it is.

```console
podman run -it --rm --entrypoint "/bin/sh" alpine -c 'echo hello && echo world'
```

This solution doesn't work with `/sbin/init`. Please re-open the issue.

```
podman run -d --name mycontainer --entrypoint "/bin/sh" alpine -c '/sbin/init && echo hello'
```

The above will result in a container that exits immediately. I don't want the container exiting. For example, if I do the following, the container stays up and running. 

```
podman run -d --name mycontainer --entrypoint "/bin/sh" alpine -c '/sbin/init'
```

But, what I want is to be able to run commands after the `/sbin/init`.

If you run a container with the init system, then the secondary script should be put into a unit file if the system is using systemd, if it is not then it should be put into an rc script that the init system launches.  Bottom line what you are trying to do  is not going to work.
